figures.caption: Fig. 17–1.An emf is induced in a loop if the flux is changed by varying the area of the circuit.
Fig. 17–2.When the disc rotates there is an emf from v×B\Figv\times\FigB, but with no change in the linked flux.
Fig. 17–3.When the plates are rocked in a uniform magnetic field, there can be a large change in the flux linkage without the generation of an emf.
Fig. 17–4.An electron accelerating in an axially symmetric, increasing magnetic field.
Fig. 17–5.Will the disc rotate if the current II is stopped?
Fig. 17–6.A coil of wire rotating in a uniform magnetic field—the basic idea of the ac generator.
Fig. 17–7.A circuit with an ac generator and a resistance.
Fig. 17–8.A current in coil 11 produces a magnetic field through coil 22.
Fig. 17–9.Any two coils have a mutual inductance M\mutualInd proportional to the integral of ds1⋅ds2/r12d\Figs_1\cdot d\Figs_2/r_{12}.
Fig. 17–10.(a) A circuit with a voltage source and an inductance. (b) An analogous mechanical system.
